## ADD — how to work in this repo

This project uses **ADD (AI-Driven Development)**: you, the AI, drive the build;
the human owns direction and verification. Before you change code:

1. Run `python3 .add/tooling/add.py status` — where the project is and what's
   next (the resume point; read it first every session).
2. Read `.add/PROJECT.md` — the foundation (domain · spec · UI/UX) every task
   builds on.
3. Let the **`add` skill drive the flow**: INTAKE sizes the request into a
   milestone, then each task runs the **one-approval front** — you draft Spec +
   Scenarios + Contract + Tests as one bundle, the human gives ONE approval at the
   frozen contract — followed by a self-driving build→verify run. `add.py` is your
   hands (scaffold + track state); the human talks to you, not the CLI.

The full method (the book) is in `.add/docs/`; the `add` skill loads the right
phase guide on demand. This block is generated by `add.py sync-guidelines`; edit
outside the markers, not inside.
<!-- ADD:END -->
